description_long: This house features a traditional half-timbered facade on the front, with exposed wooden beams and cream-colored infill panels, evoking a classic European, likely Tudor, architectural style. The roof is steeply pitched and covered with weathered, multi-toned shingles, and two prominent chimneys rise from either end. The front elevation displays symmetrical rows of windows with dark wooden frames, and the ground floor includes a double-door entry flanked by large windows. The sides and rear of the house are finished in plain, gray stucco, with fewer windows and a more utilitarian appearance. The rear elevation is more complex, with a central protruding section and additional windows, suggesting multiple rooms and a functional layout. Overall, the structure appears robust and historic, suitable for placement in a dense urban or old-town neighborhood.
description: A historic, half-timbered house with a steep shingled roof, symmetrical front windows, and a prominent double-door entry. The sides and rear are plain stucco with fewer windows, giving it a classic European urban character.
description_view_0: The front view shows the decorative half-timbered facade, symmetrical windows, and a central double-door entry.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals a plain gray stucco wall with a large chimney and no windows.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a more complex layout with a central protruding section, multiple windows, and a small rear door.
description_view_3: The right side view is similar to the left, with a plain stucco wall, a large chimney, and no windows.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows on front, sparse and irregular on sides and rear
attribute_entryway_design: Central double-door entry with large windows on either side, no porch or overhang, framed by half-timbered detailing
